title: A-Z of ancient DNA protocols for shotgun Illumina Next Generation Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2vbbh","text":"This collection contains established ancient DNA (aDNA) specific protocols for Illumina next generation sequencing. It covers sampling of skeletal remains for regions preserving higher amounts of aDNA, DNA extraction methods designed for retrieval of very short DNA fragments, and various library construction methods (including treatments for the 'removal' of typical aDNA damage). All protocols have been adapted after published protocols (see each for the corresponding original publication), and are used primarily in the Department of Archaeogenetics at the MPI-SHH (Jena, Germany).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aup69","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ioq1","text":"All protocols in this collection can be used together, and in some cases swapped out with others. All protocols prior indexing are mostly performed in a dedicated ancient DNA laboratory - please see the guidelines section of each protocol for more information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9de49","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bujjo","text":"The order of protocols is as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dh8p7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6im4i","text":"1. Sampling for aDNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"55e2u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"20sf5","text":"Sampling of Pars petrosa (os temporale, ''petrous bones') for human aDNA extraction","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":12,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dcst7","text":"Sampling of teeth for human and microbial (e.g. pathogen) aDNA extraction","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bc17i","text":"Sampling of dental calculus for oral microbiome aDNA extraction","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b0v76","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3s0mc","text":"2. Retrieval of DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"81e7e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"sg91","text":"Ancient DNA Extraction from Skeletal Material (based on the Dabney et al. 2013 Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A protocol)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":79,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":60,"length":43}],"data":[]},{"key":"4d9ji","text":"Ancient DNA extraction from dental calculus (based on the Dabney et al. 2013 Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A with extra washing steps from Ozga et al. 2016 Am J Phys Anthropol)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":77,"length":24},{"style":"italic","offset":149,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":58,"length":43},{"key":2,"offset":132,"length":36}],"data":[]},{"key":"frif0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7uukd","text":"3. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1d6lg","text":"Working in an Ancient DNA Laboratory","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6jlq6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ofu7","text":"Some of the protocols in this collection require working in dedicated ancient DNA laboratories to limit modern DNA contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":41,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"939u2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bnjqt","text":"- All steps of the protocol should take place in a clean room facility specifically designed for ancient DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d8b3e","text":"- The researcher performing lab work should wear correspondingly suitable lab-wear, such as:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6d0ag","text":"   - full-body suit with hood (e.g., Tyvek)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qun5","text":"   - hairnet","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fnkrb","text":"   - face mask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ca0ps","text":"   - two pairs of clean gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1m18a","text":"   - clean shoes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4vk1p","text":"   - protective glasses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1scno","text":"- Sample processing should be carried out in separated work benches with integrated UV irradiation (e.g. Dead Air PCR work bench)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fp5t7","text":"- Surfaces and equipment should be regularly decontaminated with e.g. bleach solution or Thermofisher's DNA AWAY (or similar) and irradiated with UV.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5nn6t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3f0t9","text":"Please see the following for more detailed guidance: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ftko","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"34psm","text":"Llamas, B. et al., 2017. From the field to the laboratory: Controlling DNA contamination in human ancient DNA research in the high-throughput sequencing era. STAR: Science \u0026 Technology of Archaeological Research, 3(1), pp.1–14. Available at: https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":242,"length":45}],"data":[]},{"key":"fc26","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E5A3DF3CE5D711EABE800A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824"}}}}
